reason: Both correctly identify the core repositioning move (drop 'AI-powered', lead with forecasting/utilization outcome, narrow to a category of one, make PM features supporting context). A states this well but stops at a single-sentence recommendation with no elaboration. B makes the same core call and adds substantive, non-padded value: it reframes the competitive set (Float/Runn instead of Asana/Monday), ties utilization directly to agency P&L/margin (a sharper articulation of why this matters to buyers), gives concrete tactical moves (homepage headline, demo order, benchmark report, coexistence strategy to lower switching cost), and offers a crisp litmus test for whether the reposition succeeded. None of this is filler — each point is decision-relevant and actionable. B is longer but earns the length with real content rather than hedging or repetition, so on usefulness it clearly exceeds A without sacrificing correctness.
